```java
return iField.getDifferenceAsLong(
```

I'm trying to understand what this change does. The ternary operator `false ? ...` will always evaluate to the second part because the condition is false. So, regardless of whether iTimeField was true or false before, now it's using `getOffsetToAdd(minuendInstant)` instead.

Wait, no—let me clarify: in Java, the ternary operator is a condition followed by two expressions. If the condition is true, it picks the first expression; if false, the second. So in this case, since the condition is `false`, it will always execute the second part of the ternary. So the code inside becomes:

minuendInstant + getOffsetToAdd(minuendInstant)

So for both cases where iTimeField was true or false before, now it's always using getOffsetToAdd on minuendInstant.
